_doc: Tier model for the 10-language PE benchmark suite. Single source of truth for which langs are in scope for which problem ranges. Consumed by coverage_report.py, three_metric_report.py, compare_languages.py, final_analysis.py via scripts/tiers.py. See project_pe_tier_model_2026-05-22 in auto-memory for rationale.

tier_1_foundation.label: Foundation
tier_1_foundation.description: All 10 languages; the apples-to-apples cross-language comparison surface.

tier_2_deep_coverage.label: Deep Coverage
tier_2_deep_coverage.description: Languages we've intentionally pushed past 200; the 5-lang comparison surface for the 201-300 band. Range was briefly expanded to 201-400 on 2026-05-25 then narrowed back to 201-300 on 2026-05-26 — python and zig are explicitly NOT extended into 301+ (python out-of-gas at level 5+, zig deliberately uninteresting for tier-3 work). This is the natural ceiling for Deep Coverage; tier 3 takes over above 300 with a tighter 3-lang trio.

tier_3_frontier.label: Frontier
tier_3_frontier.description: C++, Go, and Rust as the verification trio for unexplored problems. Rust promoted into the trio on 2026-05-26 — 3-way cross-language agreement is strictly stronger than 2-way (harder to fall into a shared-misreading trap like the 2026-05-25 p254 incident). Lower bound moved 401 → 301 on 2026-05-26 to reframe 301-400 from 'tier-2 extension' to 'tier-3 build-out' where rust is the natural ceiling-setting lang.

_historical_exceptions_note: Languages may have committed solutions beyond their highest tier's problem_range (e.g., Rust has 36 problems above 200). Those stay preserved per the historical-exception convention in project_200_cap_policy auto-memory, but do NOT count for tier-comparison stats. Reports show them only in the lang's own data, not in cross-tier rankings.
